9,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

24,	"OUT-OF-POCKET COSTS","CONCEPT","The actual expenses incurred by Medicare beneficiaries that are not covered by insurance and must be paid directly."
25,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

2,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

24,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

8,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

6,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

id,	entity,	type,	description
1,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

16,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

31,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

26,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

34,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

7,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

id,	entity,	type,	description
1,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

11,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

1,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government

2,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

results.fact: If PDP Sponsor does not renew a contract, CMS cannot enter into a contract with an organization whose covered persons (as defined in 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(4)) also served as covered persons for the nonrenewing PDP Sponsor for 2 years in the affected PDP region(s), unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration.

If a PDP Sponsor does not renew any of its plan benefit packages (PBPs) in a PDP region, CMS does not approve plan bids submitted by the organization in that PDP region for 2 years, unless CMS determines there are circumstances that warrant special consideration, per 42 CFR § 423.507(a)(3).

8,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

4,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

8,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

2,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

5,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

It has the authority to terminate or non-renew a PDP Sponsor's contract if the waiver expiration leads to ineligibility to offer a PDP plan in any region."<SEP>"CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the federal agency responsible for administering Medicare, including the authority to terminate contracts with PDP Sponsors under specific regulations."<SEP>"CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the federal agency responsible for approving notices and scripts."<SEP>"CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the federal agency that contracts with an entity to track Medicare beneficiaries' true out-of-pocket costs and oversees the PDP Sponsor's compliance."<SEP>"CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the federal agency that establishes fees and regulations for cost sharing in the Medicare Part D program."<SEP>"CMS agrees to perform its obligations to the PDP Sponsor in accordance with the Act and related regulations."<SEP>"CMS has the authority to assess compliance and initiate enforcement actions against PDP Sponsors."<SEP>"CMS is a federal agency that administers Medicare and enters into contracts with PDP Sponsors."<SEP>"CMS is the federal agency responsible for releasing public data on Part D programs, in compliance with Subpart K of 42 CFR Part 423."<SEP>"CMS refers to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, a U.S. federal agency responsible for administering Medicare programs, including the Medicare Advantage Attestation of Benefit Plan. It is referenced in contracting materials and is updated annually to reflect PDP Sponsor's approved bids for the next contract year."<SEP>"Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, the federal agency overseeing Medicare programs."<SEP>"Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, which has authority to remove regions from a sponsor’s service area."<SEP>"The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, the federal agency responsible for administering Medicare programs."<SEP>"The federal agency that contracts with an entity to track out-of-pocket costs for Medicare beneficiaries."<SEP>"The federal agency that receives and monitors complaints against PDP Sponsors."
2,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

8,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.

29,	"PDP SPONSOR","ORGANIZATION",The PDP Sponsor is a private entity approved by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to operate a Voluntary Medicare Prescription Drug Plan (PDP) under Medicare Part D. As a federally regulated organization, the PDP Sponsor must meet stringent federal and state requirements to maintain eligibility, including obtaining and maintaining valid waivers and adhering to all CMS regulations. The PDP Sponsor is responsible for administering the Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plan, which includes calculating and collecting premiums from beneficiaries in accordance with 42 CFR §§ 423.286 and 423.293, submitting approved bids annually, and updating drug reimbursement standards at least every seven days starting January 1 to reflect current market prices.

The PDP Sponsor agrees to enter into a business associate agreement with any entity contracted by CMS to track Medicare beneficiaries’ true out-of-pocket costs and is required to comply with payment obligations established by CMS for cost-sharing related to enrollment, as mandated by 42 CFR § 423.6. The entity must also comply with all applicable federal laws and regulations, including those related to fraud, waste, and abuse, such as the False Claims Act and anti-kickback provisions, and must adhere to anti-discrimination laws and regulations.

The PDP Sponsor is bound by contractual obligations, including a two-year restriction on reapplying to offer plans in a region after terminating its contract with CMS, as outlined in 42 CFR § 423.510. While the PDP Sponsor has the right to choose not to renew its contract for any reason, provided proper notice is given, it may only terminate its contract under the specific conditions defined by CMS. Additionally, the PDP Sponsor must attest to not altering the original contract terms presented by CMS and agrees that any modifications made by the Sponsor are not binding.

The PDP Sponsor is responsible for addressing and resolving complaints received by CMS and is subject to enforcement actions if found out of compliance with Part D requirements. The entity operates within the healthcare and prescription drug services sector and may be subject to investigations, legal actions, or arbitration by government agencies related to payments from federal and state entities. Overall, the PDP Sponsor is an organization determined eligible by CMS to sponsor and administer Medicare Part D Prescription Drug Plans, operating under strict regulatory oversight and contractual commitments.
